summary: Observed Explorer runtime setting for hiding empty drives. Microsoft Learn describes hideDrivesWithNoMedia as a File Explorer Classic advanced setting, the 25H2 raw registry dump lists HideDrivesWithNoMedia under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch, the app now writes the same current-user value through VisibilityTweakProvider, and a reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\HideDrivesWithNoMedia with both Data: 0 and Data: 1 after Explorer restart.

setting.name: Hide Empty Drives
setting.casual_explanation: Explorer can hide or show drives that do not currently have media present.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable this when they want Explorer to look cleaner by hiding empty removable-drive slots, or disable it when they want every drive slot visible.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This is a low-risk Explorer preference. Enabling it hides empty drives from Explorer views, while disabling it keeps all drive slots visible.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Learn describes hideDrivesWithNoMedia as a File Explorer Classic advanced setting. The 25H2 raw registry dump lists HideDrivesWithNoMedia under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch, corroborating that the value family is still present on current builds. A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon probe on 2026-03-24 then confirmed live Explorer consumption: after writing HideDrivesWithNoMedia = 0 and restarting Explorer into This PC, Explorer.EXE queried the value with Data: 0; after writing HideDrivesWithNoMedia = 1 and restarting Explorer into This PC, Explorer.EXE queried the same value with Data: 1. The app now maps the same path and value through VisibilityTweakProvider, so the shipped apply surface matches the validated runtime preference.

allowed_values.label: Show empty drives
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer keeps drives with no media visible. The Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 0 after restart into This PC.

allowed_values.label: Hide empty drives
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer hides drives with no media present. The Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 1 after restart into This PC.

targets.notes: The Microsoft settings reference describes the feature, while the 25H2 dump and Procmon probe corroborate the direct registry value and live Explorer read path.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now exposes HideDrivesWithNoMedia through VisibilityTweakProvider and writes the same HKCU Explorer\Advanced value that the runtime probe validated.

recommended_profiles.label: Hide empty drives
intended_for: Users who want Explorer to look cleaner
avoid_for: Users who want all drive slots visible

states.rationale: This matches the runtime-observed Explorer read for the hidden state.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer to hide empty removable-drive slots.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to see all drive slots even when they are empty.
tradeoffs: Cleans up Explorer views by hiding empty drives.
Removes visibility of empty drive slots.

recommended_profiles.label: Show empty drives
intended_for: Users who want all drive slots visible
avoid_for: Users who prefer a cleaner This PC view

states.rationale: This matches the runtime-observed Explorer read for the visible state.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer to show all drive slots, even when empty.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want empty drives hidden from view.
tradeoffs: Keeps empty drive slots visible in Explorer.
Makes This PC a little busier.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: settings-common File Explorer Classic advanced settings
evidence.summary: Microsoft Learn describes hideDrivesWithNoMedia as a File Explorer Classic advanced setting and says it indicates whether Explorer should hide drives with no media present.

evidence.title: 25H2 raw registry corroboration for HideDrivesWithNoMedia
evidence.summary: The 25H2 raw registry dump lists HideDrivesWithNoMedia under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch, corroborating that the value family is still present on current builds.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer HideDrivesWithNoMedia runtime surface
evidence.summary: A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 wrote HideDrivesWithNoMedia = 0 and HideDrivesWithNoMedia = 1, restarted Explorer into This PC after each write, and captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\HideDrivesWithNoMedia with matching Data: 0 and Data: 1 values.

evidence.title: Current app implementation for HideDrivesWithNoMedia
evidence.summary: VisibilityTweakProvider now exposes explorer.hide-empty-drives and writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\HideDrivesWithNoMedia = 1 for the current user.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the feature, the 25H2 dump corroborates the HideDrivesWithNoMedia value family, the Win25H2Clean Procmon trace confirms live Explorer consumption of both states, and the current app implementation now writes the same validated value. This closes the loop from current-build corroboration to runtime behavior to shipped app mapping.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: STATE=0 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\HideDrivesWithNoMedia ... Data: 0; STATE=1 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\HideDrivesWithNoMedia ... Data: 1; RESTORED_EXISTS=False
validation_proof.notes: The guest-local result file was copied back to the host scratch area during validation. The baseline value was absent and was restored to the absent state after the probe.
